title: lophomyrtus × ralphii
intro: Lophomyrtus × ralphii, a hybrid plant species, is a cross between Lophomyrtus bullata and Myrtus ralphii. It is native to New Zealand and has been used in traditional Māori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and aiding in wound healing. In herbal preparations, Lophomyrtus × ralphii is often used in teas, infusions, and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
